evals.prompt: I have the SNP rs7903146 near TCF7L2. What phenotypes is it associated with, and does the association replicate across European and East Asian populations?
evals.expected_output: A cross-biobank PheWAS report identifying Type 2 diabetes as the top, replicated association across UKB-TOPMed (European), BioBank Japan (Japanese), and TPMI (Taiwanese) with concordant positive effect direction, noting the weaker TPMI p-value reflects lower case count rather than weaker biology.

assertions.text: Calls UKBTOPMed_phewas_by_variant and at least one East-Asian biobank tool (BioBankJapan_phewas_by_variant or TPMI_phewas_by_variant) for rs7903146
Identifies Type 2 diabetes / diabetes mellitus as the top association
Concludes the association replicates across ancestries (concordant direction), not from memory but from the returned p-values

evals.prompt: What happens phenotypically when you knock out PCSK9? I'm interested in rare loss-of-function burden, not common variants.
evals.expected_output: A gene-burden PheWAS via Genebass_gene_burden_phewas(gene='PCSK9', burden_set='pLoF') reporting lipid/LDL-cholesterol phenotypes as the strongest burden associations, correctly using the gene-level rare-variant tool rather than a single-variant lookup.

assertions.text: Calls Genebass_gene_burden_phewas with gene PCSK9 and a loss-of-function burden_set (pLoF)
Reports LDL / cholesterol / lipid phenotypes as top burden associations
Does NOT treat this as a single-variant lookup (recognizes the rare-burden framing)

evals.prompt: Is the association of rs671 (ALDH2) with alcohol-related traits something seen only in East Asians, or does it show up in Europeans too?
evals.expected_output: A cross-biobank comparison noting rs671 is common in East Asian biobanks (BBJ/TPMI) but rare/monomorphic in Europeans, so the apparent East-Asian specificity is driven largely by allele frequency/power — the skill should check af/num_cases before concluding biology.

assertions.text: Queries both an East-Asian biobank (BBJ or TPMI) and UKB-TOPMed for rs671
Invokes the allele-frequency / power caveat (checks af or notes the variant is rare in Europeans) rather than declaring pure biological specificity
